checks.evidence: strict matrix reports overall_pass, no generated nonce reuse, no silent corruption, and no unexpected liveness failures
checks.name: strict_matrix_overall

checks.evidence: partial update/remount returns latest committed state with no generated duplicate journal pairs
checks.name: strict_partial_update_remount

checks.evidence: torn strict journal tail is ignored and remount exposes the latest committed payload
checks.name: strict_torn_record_tail

checks.evidence: daemon kill after generation reservation/data fsync skips the unpublished generation on remount
checks.name: strict_interrupted_generation_advance

checks.evidence: adversarial append of an older mapping creates a replay duplicate but does not supersede the newer mapping
checks.name: strict_replayed_metadata

checks.evidence: file-backed stale snapshot remains replayable, while retained TPM replay-after-advance artifact fails closed
checks.name: strict_stale_checkpoint_ladder

checks.evidence: epoch replay matrix reports all cases pass
checks.name: epoch_matrix_overall

checks.evidence: epoch committed-prefix replay ignores a torn log tail and recovers matching data
checks.name: epoch_torn_log_tail

checks.evidence: epoch replay rejects duplicate generation records instead of exposing data
checks.name: epoch_duplicate_append_fail_closed

checks.evidence: epoch replay repairs a deliberately lost strict journal from the committed epoch prefix
checks.name: epoch_stale_journal_repair

checks.evidence: normal epoch remount compacts the committed prefix without duplicate generations
checks.name: epoch_normal_committed_prefix

checks.evidence: nonce/AAD bind file_id, block, generation; strict replay chooses highest committed generation; epoch replay rejects duplicate keys
checks.name: source_invariant_guard

checks.evidence: paper states duplicate-generation invariant and residual power-loss/PCR/crash-proof limits
checks.name: paper_negative_claim_guard

claim_boundary: C3 proves nonce/generation safety only for the retained final-binary strict and epoch fault models. Strict evidence is per-file sidecar (block,generation) evidence; epoch replay additionally checks (file_id,block,generation) duplicate records. This does not prove physical power-loss, kernel-crash, drive-cache, PCR-bound rollback resistance, or complete POSIX crash certification.
